description_long: This house is a rectangular stone structure with a steeply pitched gable roof covered in gray slate tiles. The exterior walls are constructed from rough-hewn stone blocks, giving it a historic and sturdy appearance. Windows are small, rectangular, and irregularly spaced, with some appearing to be shuttered or boarded up. The main entryway is a simple wooden door, set flush with the wall and lacking decorative elements, suggesting a utilitarian design. The building sits on a grassy plot, with minimal landscaping and no visible porches or overhangs. The overall look is reminiscent of traditional rural or medieval English architecture, emphasizing function over ornamentation.
description: A rectangular stone house with a steep gable roof, small irregular windows, and a simple wooden door, set on a grassy base.
description_view_0: End view showing the twin gable roof peaks and a plain stone wall with three small slit windows near the base.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the main facade with a central wooden door, several small windows, and a stone path leading to the entrance.
description_view_2: Opposite end view mirroring view 0, with a similar gable roof and three small slit windows at the base.
description_view_3: Rear side view featuring a single door, several small windows, and a less uniform stone pattern compared to the front.
attribute_entryway_design: Simple wooden door flush with the wall, no porch or decorative elements, stone path leading to entrance
